Front:
**1.** Under P.G. 200-02, a sergeant who receives a report of corruption or serious misconduct must notify:

A) A) The desk officer only
B) B) Internal Affairs Bureau Command Center
C) C) The patrol borough commander
D) D) The Chief of Personnel
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 200-02

P.G. 200-02 imposes an absolute duty on all members to report corruption or serious misconduct to the IAB Command Center, which operates 24 hours.

Front:
**2.** A member of the service has a duty to intervene to stop excessive force. Failure to intervene may result in:

A) A) A verbal reprimand
B) B) Command discipline only
C) C) Criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to dismissal
D) D) Transfer to another command
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-02

P.G. 221-02 states that failure to intervene or report excessive force may result in criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to and including dismissal.

Front:
**3.** A CEW discharge in cartridge mode is classified as what level force incident?

A) A) Level 1
B) B) Level 2
C) C) Level 3
D) D) Level 4
Back:
**Answer: A**

Source: P.G. 221-03

P.G. 221-03 classifies a CEW discharge in cartridge mode as a Level 1 force incident, requiring investigation by the immediate supervisor.

Front:
**4.** The immediate supervisor in a force incident must be:

A) A) Any available supervisor from the same command
B) B) A supervisor not involved in the incident, at least one rank higher than all involved members
C) C) The desk officer of the precinct of occurrence
D) D) The duty captain in all cases
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-03

P.G. 221-03 defines the immediate supervisor as one NOT involved in the incident and at least one rank higher than all involved members.

Front:
**5.** O.C. pepper spray should be applied using:

A) A) One continuous burst at two feet
B) B) Two, one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet
C) C) Three short bursts at five feet
D) D) One long burst at arm's length
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-07

P.G. 221-07 specifies O.C. pepper spray must be applied in two, one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet.

Front:
**6.** What is the recommended minimum "zone of safety" distance from an EDP?

A) A) 10 feet
B) B) 15 feet
C) C) 20 feet
D) D) 25 feet
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 defines the zone of safety as greater than the effective range of any weapon other than a firearm, with a minimum of 20 feet.

Front:
**7.** Who investigates Level 3 force incidents?

A) A) Immediate Supervisor
B) B) Commanding Officer/Executive Officer
C) C) Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Force Investigation Division
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-03

P.G. 221-03 assigns investigation of Level 3 force incidents to the Internal Affairs Bureau.

Front:
**8.** The UMOS Report of Firearms Discharge (PD324-140) must be completed within:

A) A) 24 hours
B) B) 48 hours
C) C) 72 hours
D) D) End of tour
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-04

P.G. 221-04 requires the UMOS Report of Firearms Discharge (PD324-140) to be completed within 48 hours.

Front:
**9.** After a firearms discharge causing death or injury, the involved UMOS is temporarily assigned for a minimum of:

A) A) One scheduled tour
B) B) Three consecutive scheduled tours
C) C) Five consecutive scheduled tours
D) D) Seven calendar days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-04

P.G. 221-04 mandates temporary assignment for a minimum of three consecutive scheduled tours after a firearms discharge causing death or injury.

Front:
**10.** Which of the following is a firearm prohibition under P.G. 221-01?

A) A) Discharging a firearm at a fleeing felon who presents an imminent threat
B) B) Firing warning shots
C) C) Discharging a firearm to protect from imminent physical injury
D) D) Discharging a firearm in self-defense
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-01

P.G. 221-01 explicitly lists firing warning shots as a firearm prohibition under all circumstances.

Front:
**11.** Members SHALL NOT discharge firearms at or from a moving vehicle unless:

A) A) The vehicle is being used in a pursuit
B) B) The vehicle is stolen
C) C) Deadly physical force is being used by means other than a moving vehicle
D) D) The vehicle is approaching a checkpoint
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-01

P.G. 221-01 prohibits discharge at or from a moving vehicle unless deadly physical force is being used by means other than the moving vehicle.

Front:
**12.** Who may apply a spit hood to an EDP?

A) A) Any uniformed member of the service
B) B) The patrol supervisor
C) C) Only Emergency Service Unit personnel
D) D) EMS personnel only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 restricts application of a spit hood exclusively to ESU personnel.

Front:
**13.** An EDP must NEVER be transported to:

A) A) The nearest hospital
B) B) A police facility
C) C) A psychiatric ward
D) D) An ambulance
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 states that under no circumstances shall an EDP be transported to a police facility.

Front:
**14.** The response of ESU to an EDP scene may only be canceled by:

A) A) Any member of the service
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) A uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above
D) D) The precinct commanding officer only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 provides that ESU response to an EDP scene may only be canceled by a uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above.

Front:
**15.** How many T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORTS are prepared per force incident?

A) A) One per involved member
B) B) One per subject
C) C) Only ONE per incident, regardless of number of MOS or subjects
D) D) One per force level
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-03

P.G. 221-03 establishes that only ONE T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT is prepared per force incident, regardless of the number of MOS or subjects.

Front:
**16.** For Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents, the immediate supervisor should handle BWC footage by:

A) A) Reviewing all videos and documenting findings
B) B) Uploading footage to FORMS T.R.I. application
C) C) Safeguarding cameras and NOT viewing — await IAB/FID direction
D) D) Forwarding footage to the commanding officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-03

For Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents, the immediate supervisor must safeguard BWC cameras and NOT view footage, awaiting IAB/FID direction.

Front:
**17.** CEWs should be carried in a holster on which side?

A) A) Shooting side, same as firearm
B) B) Support side, opposite the firearm
C) C) Either side at the member's preference
D) D) Ankle holster
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-08

P.G. 221-08 requires CEWs to be carried on the support side, opposite the firearm, and prohibits cross-drawing.

Front:
**18.** A "Resisting Recidivist" is defined as a prisoner arrested for charges against MOS:

A) A) Three or more times in ten years
B) B) More than once within five years
C) C) Twice within one year
D) D) Any repeat offense regardless of timeframe
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-06

P.G. 221-06 defines a Resisting Recidivist as a prisoner arrested for charges against MOS more than once within five years.

Front:
**19.** Vehicle checkpoints should NOT exceed what duration at one location?

A) A) 1 hour
B) B) 2 hours
C) C) 3 hours
D) D) 4 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-16

P.G. 221-16 specifies vehicle checkpoints should not exceed two hours at one location.

Front:
**20.** Department canine teams shall NOT be used for:

A) A) Tracking missing persons
B) B) Crowd control or deterrent at peaceful demonstrations
C) C) Searching buildings for break-in suspects
D) D) Performing cadaver searches
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-18

P.G. 221-18 expressly prohibits Department canine teams from being used for crowd control or as a deterrent at peaceful demonstrations.

Front:
**21.** The use of distraction devices requires approval from:

A) A) Patrol borough commander
B) B) Duty inspector
C) C) Chief of Department or designee
D) D) Chief of Patrol
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-11

P.G. 221-11 requires approval from the Chief of Department or designee before distraction devices may be used.

Front:
**22.** Who has the authority to approve the use of tear gas?

A) A) Precinct commanding officer
B) B) Patrol borough commander or designee
C) C) Chief of Department
D) D) Emergency Service Unit supervisor
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-09

P.G. 221-09 vests the authority to approve tear gas use with the patrol borough commander or designee.

Front:
**23.** A CEW standard cycle is how many seconds?

A) A) 3 seconds
B) B) 5 seconds
C) C) 10 seconds
D) D) 15 seconds
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-08

P.G. 221-08 states the standard CEW cycle is five seconds.

Front:
**24.** At the scene of a firearms discharge, the PBT alcohol test threshold is:

A) A) .04
B) B) .05
C) C) .08
D) D) .10
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-23

P.G. 221-23 establishes that a PBT reading of .08 or greater requires transport to the IDTU facility for an Intoxilyzer test.

Front:
**25.** When a prisoner is found unconscious in a cell, the first member to discover the condition must:

A) A) Notify the desk officer immediately
B) B) Enter the cell and begin emergency care
C) C) Wait for a supervisor before entering
D) D) Contact EMS only
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 210-09

P.G. 210-09 directs that the discovering member must enter the cell immediately and begin emergency care when a prisoner is found unconscious.

Front:
**26.** A uniformed member of the service who misuses a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption will be:

A) A) Placed on modified assignment
B) B) Suspended for 30 days
C) C) Terminated from the Department
D) D) Given command discipline
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A.G. 304-04 mandates termination for any UMOS who misuses a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption.

Front:
**27.** A UMOS causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit due to alcohol. The consequence is:

A) A) Dismissal Probation only
B) B) Suspension and retraining
C) C) Termination, absent exigent circumstances
D) D) Modified assignment for 30 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A.G. 304-04 requires termination when a UMOS causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit due to alcohol, absent exigent circumstances.

Front:
**28.** The Counseling Services Unit operates:

A) A) Monday through Friday, 0900-1700 hours
B) B) 24 hours a day, seven days a week
C) C) Only during business hours
D) D) By appointment only
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A.G. 304-04 specifies that the Counseling Services Unit operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

Front:
**29.** A member participating in counseling programs for alcohol should know that:

A) A) Their promotional opportunities will be jeopardized
B) B) They will automatically be transferred
C) C) Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities
D) D) They will be placed on restricted duty
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A.G. 304-04 expressly states that participation in counseling programs will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities.

Front:
**30.** The Counseling Services Unit maintains confidentiality under:

A) A) New York State Civil Service Law
B) B) Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs
C) C) Department Administrative Guide only
D) D) HIPAA regulations
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A.G. 304-04 states the Counseling Services Unit maintains confidentiality pursuant to Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs.

Front:
**31.** All firearm repairs and modifications must be performed by:

A) A) Any certified gunsmith
B) B) The member's commanding officer
C) C) Firearms and Tactics Section armorers solely
D) D) The member with CO approval
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 requires all firearm repairs and modifications to be performed solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers.

Front:
**32.** Which type of holster is prohibited?

A) A) Shoulder holster
B) B) Ankle holster
C) C) Belly band holster
D) D) Inside-waistband holster
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 specifically lists belly band holsters among prohibited carrying methods.

Front:
**33.** Firearms may NOT be stored or left in:

A) A) A locked firearms safe at home
B) B) An unattended motor vehicle
C) C) The desk officer's safekeeping
D) D) A firearms safety station
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 prohibits storing or leaving firearms in an unattended motor vehicle.

Front:
**34.** A UMOS entering Family Court must be:

A) A) Armed with service pistol only
B) B) Unarmed, unless present as arresting officer or on official business
C) C) Armed with both service and off-duty weapons
D) D) Armed but with weapon concealed
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 requires members to be unarmed when entering Family Court, unless present as the arresting officer or on official business.

Front:
**35.** A report for lost or damaged uniform must be filed within:

A) A) 10 days
B) B) 30 days
C) C) 60 days
D) D) 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 305-05

A.G. 305-05 requires a report for lost or damaged uniform to be submitted within 30 days of the incident.

Front:
**36.** Reimbursement for uniform items costing less than $100 should be forwarded:

A) A) Through channels
B) B) Direct to Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget
C) C) Via Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Through the Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 305-05

A.G. 305-05 directs reimbursement claims under $100 to be forwarded directly to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget.

Front:
**37.** The maximum reimbursement amount for a lost watch is:

A) A) $50.00
B) B) $75.00
C) C) $100.00
D) D) $150.00
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 305-05

A.G. 305-05 sets the maximum reimbursement for a lost watch at $75.00.

Front:
**38.** All settlements for uniform reimbursement must be executed within:

A) A) 90 days of approval
B) B) 120 days of approval
C) C) 180 days of approval
D) D) One year of approval
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 305-05

A.G. 305-05 states all uniform reimbursement settlements must be executed within 180 days of approval or they lapse.

Front:
**39.** Upon observing a UMOS who appears intoxicated, the supervisory member must prepare PD469-150:

A) A) Within 24 hours
B) B) By end of tour
C) C) Immediately
D) D) Within 48 hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-10

A.G. 318-10 directs the supervisory member to immediately prepare PD469-150 upon observing a UMOS who appears intoxicated.

Front:
**40.** When a UMOS is determined intoxicated, the CO/XO/duty captain should:

A) A) Issue a verbal warning and send member home
B) B) Remove firearms, place on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling
C) C) Notify Internal Affairs Bureau only
D) D) Contact the Police Commissioner directly
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-10

A.G. 318-10 requires the CO/XO/duty captain to remove firearms, place the member on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling.

Front:
**41.** The Typed Letterhead report for an intoxicated UMOS is prepared in how many copies?

A) A) Five
B) B) Six
C) C) Eight
D) D) Ten
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-10

The Typed Letterhead report for an intoxicated UMOS is prepared in 8 copies addressed to the 1st Deputy Commissioner.

Front:
**42.** Prior to questioning a member in an official investigation, the interrogating officer must provide:

A) A) CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121)
B) B) NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)
C) C) SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)
D) D) Miranda Warning Card
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-11

Before questioning a member, the interrogating officer must provide the NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121).

Front:
**43.** A member under investigation has the right to view which recording prior to questioning?

A) A) Any CCTV footage from the precinct
B) B) Their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident
C) C) All BWC recordings from all officers at the scene
D) D) No recordings may be viewed
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-11

A.G. 318-11 grants a member the right to view their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident prior to questioning.

Front:
**44.** A UMOS who refuses to answer questions specifically related to official duties will be:

A) A) Given a verbal reprimand
B) B) Placed on modified assignment
C) C) Subject to suspension from duty
D) D) Charged with insubordination only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-11

A.G. 318-11 requires members to answer questions related to official duties, and refusal results in suspension from duty.

Front:
**45.** Under the Garrity warning, a member's statements during an official investigation:

A) A) Can be used in both criminal and departmental proceedings
B) B) Cannot be used in criminal proceedings but CAN be used in departmental charges
C) C) Cannot be used in either criminal or departmental proceedings
D) D) Can be used in criminal proceedings but NOT departmental charges
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-11

Under Garrity, compelled statements cannot be used in criminal proceedings but remain available for departmental charges.

Front:
**46.** An interrogation after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS must be completed at least how many days before trial?

A) A) 5 days
B) B) 10 days
C) C) 15 days
D) D) 20 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-11

A.G. 318-11 requires interrogation after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS to be completed at least 10 days prior to the Department trial date.

Front:
**47.** Which of the following is a prohibited interrogation tactic?

A) A) Recording the interrogation mechanically
B) B) Having a line organization representative present
C) C) Promises of reward for answering questions
D) D) Scheduling interrogation during business hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-11

A.G. 318-11 explicitly prohibits promises of reward for answering questions, along with off-the-record questions and threats of punishment.

Front:
**48.** Questions and answers from an interrogation may be released outside the Department only with written approval of:

A) A) The Police Commissioner
B) B) The Chief of Department
C) C) The Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters
D) D) The Chief of Internal Affairs
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-11

Interrogation questions and answers may only be released outside the Department with written approval of the Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters.

Front:
**49.** A civilian member must report an on-duty injury no later than:

A) A) 24 hours
B) B) 3 days
C) C) 7 days
D) D) 30 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 319-08

A.G. 319-08 requires a civilian member to report an on-duty injury no later than 7 days after the incident.

Front:
**50.** Who grants leave with pay for the first week's absence of a civilian member covered by Workers Compensation?

A) A) The commanding officer
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) The Chief of Personnel
D) D) The Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 319-08

A.G. 319-08 states the Chief of Personnel grants leave with pay for the first week's absence of a civilian member covered by Workers Compensation.

Front:
**51.** A Tier 2 member must report to the Police Pension Fund:

A) A) At least 10 calendar days in advance
B) B) At least 30 calendar days in advance
C) C) Without notice
D) D) At least 60 calendar days in advance
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 requires Tier 2 members to report to the Police Pension Fund at least 30 calendar days in advance of their discontinuance date.

Front:
**52.** A LEOSA Certification Card expires:

A) A) 6 months from last range requalification
B) B) 12 months from last range requalification
C) C) 24 months from retirement
D) D) It does not expire
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 specifies the LEOSA Certification Card expires 12 months from the date of last range requalification.

Front:
**53.** After commencement of terminal leave:

A) A) Member may be granted vacation
B) B) Member may be placed on sick report for any illness
C) C) No leaves of any kind may be granted
D) D) Member may request comp time
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 states that after commencement of terminal leave, no leaves of any kind may be granted.

Front:
**54.** Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by:

A) A) The Chief of Department within 6 months
B) B) The Police Commissioner within one year
C) C) The First Deputy Commissioner within 2 years
D) D) The Chief of Personnel within 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 329-07

A.G. 329-07 provides that resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year.

Front:
**55.** Tier 2 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their:

A) A) 60th birthday
B) B) 62nd birthday
C) C) 63rd birthday
D) D) 65th birthday
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 mandates Tier 2 members retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday.

Front:
**56.** A resigning member's firearms must be delivered to:

A) A) Manhattan Property Clerk
B) B) Firearms and Tactics Section
C) C) Borough Property Clerk's Office only
D) D) The member's permanent command
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 329-07

A.G. 329-07 requires a resigning member to deliver firearms to the Borough Property Clerk's Office only.

Front:
**57.** Members on terminal leave are available for duty only during:

A) A) Regular scheduled tours
B) B) Emergency situations
C) C) Programmatic overtime
D) D) Detail assignments
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 states members on terminal leave are available for duty only during emergency situations.

Front:
**58.** The investigating supervisor for a LOD injury signs off on the report:

A) A) Within 24 hours
B) B) Within 48 hours
C) C) Prior to completion of tour
D) D) By end of the following tour
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires the investigating supervisor to sign off on the LOD injury report prior to completion of tour.

Front:
**59.** For a LOD injury outside NYC, the investigating supervisor must be:

A) A) Sergeant or above
B) B) Lieutenant or above
C) C) Captain or above
D) D) Inspector or above
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires the investigating supervisor for a LOD injury outside NYC to be in the rank of captain or above.

Front:
**60.** The desk officer notifies Sick Desk for a LOD injury when:

A) A) The injury occurred during any part of the tour
B) B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour
C) C) The member was treated at the hospital
D) D) The injury was witnessed by multiple members
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires the desk officer to notify Sick Desk when the LOD injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour.

Front:
**61.** When a UMOS is treated for a LOD injury, which address goes in hospital records?

A) A) Member's home address
B) B) Precinct of occurrence
C) C) Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza
D) D) Member's permanent command
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 mandates that only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza) be entered in hospital records for LOD injuries.

Front:
**62.** A member struck by a bullet on a protective vest and apparently uninjured must:

A) A) Continue duty without evaluation
B) B) Be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury
C) C) Be placed on sick report for 24 hours
D) D) Report to Medical Division the following day
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires a member struck by a bullet on a protective vest to be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury.

Front:
**63.** The Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting occurs:

A) A) 24 hours after the event
B) B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact
C) C) One week after the event
D) D) 30 days after the event
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-07

A.G. 330-07 schedules the Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting at 48 hours after the event or initial contact.

Front:
**64.** All conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are:

A) A) Reported to the commanding officer
B) B) Recorded in the Command Log
C) C) Strictly confidential
D) D) Shared with Internal Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-07

A.G. 330-07 provides that all conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are strictly confidential.

Front:
**65.** The Trauma Counseling Team responds to all LOD incidents plus any incident requested by:

A) A) The desk officer
B) B) A captain or above
C) C) Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) The patrol supervisor
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-07

A.G. 330-07 states the Trauma Counseling Team responds to all LOD incidents plus any incident requested by a captain or above.

Front:
**66.** The bleach solution for cleaning blood spills is:

A) A) 1 part bleach to 5 parts water
B) B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water
C) C) 1 part bleach to 20 parts water
D) D) Undiluted bleach
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-09

A.G. 330-09 specifies blood spills must be cleaned with a freshly mixed solution of 1 part bleach to 10 parts water.

Front:
**67.** Hypodermic needles should be handled using:

A) A) Two-handed grip technique
B) B) One-handed "scoop" technique
C) C) Pliers and container method
D) D) Gloved removal technique
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-09

A.G. 330-09 requires hypodermic needles to be placed in a sharps container using the one-handed scoop technique.

Front:
**68.** To avoid unnecessary exposure at a scene, control of a person suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to:

A) A) The patrol supervisor
B) B) EMS personnel only
C) C) An officer who has already had contact with the person
D) D) The next available officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-09

A.G. 330-09 states control of a person suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to an officer who has already had contact.

Front:
**69.** Bleach solution for blood spills should be disposed of in:

A) A) Any available drain
B) B) A urinal or toilet
C) C) A sink only
D) D) A hazardous waste container
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-09

A.G. 330-09 requires bleach solution to be disposed of in a sink only, not a urinal or toilet which may contain reactive deodorizers.

Front:
**70.** Under CIMS, the recommended span of control is:

A) A) 1 to 3
B) B) 3 to 7
C) C) 5 to 10
D) D) 10 to 15
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-01

P.G. 220-01 establishes the CIMS recommended span of control as 3 to 7 for general use.

Front:
**71.** In the CIMS Incident Command structure, the FIRST priority is always:

A) A) Incident stabilization
B) B) Life safety
C) C) Property conservation
D) D) Evidence preservation
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-01

P.G. 220-01 establishes Life Safety as the immediate first priority under CIMS, superseding all other objectives.

Front:
**72.** A Unified Command is used when:

A) A) Only one agency responds
B) B) Multiple agencies or jurisdictions are involved
C) C) The incident is minor
D) D) The Police Commissioner directs
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-01

P.G. 220-01 provides that Unified Command is used when multiple agencies or jurisdictions share primary authority over an incident.

Front:
**73.** Under P.G. 207-17, when a citizen wishes to make a complaint against a member of the service, the desk officer must:

A) A) Discourage the complaint if minor
B) B) Accept the complaint and prepare a COMPLAINT REPORT
C) C) Refer the citizen to CCRB or IAB only
D) D) Require the citizen to return during business hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 207-17

The desk officer is required to accept any complaint a citizen wishes to make against a member of the service and prepare a COMPLAINT REPORT.

Front:
**74.** When arresting a juvenile (under 16), the arresting officer must:

A) A) Handcuff the juvenile in all cases
B) B) Not handcuff unless necessary for safety
C) C) Transport in an RMP with adult prisoners
D) D) Process at central booking
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 215-01

Department policy is to avoid handcuffing juveniles unless necessary for safety.

Front:
**75.** A desk officer who receives notification of an arrest must ensure the prisoner is searched by:

A) A) Any available officer
B) B) A member of the same sex as the prisoner
C) C) The arresting officer only
D) D) A supervisor
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 208-03

P.G. 208-03 requires the facility search to be conducted by a member of the same sex as the prisoner.

Front:
**76.** Prisoners classified as emotionally disturbed must be:

A) A) Placed in a cell with other prisoners
B) B) Kept under constant supervision
C) C) Released to EMS immediately
D) D) Transported to central booking
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 210-04

P.G. 210-04 requires emotionally disturbed prisoners to be kept under constant supervision.

Front:
**77.** When property is recovered at a crime scene, the first officer must:

A) A) Move property to a secure location
B) B) Safeguard the property in place until detective arrival
C) C) Voucher the property immediately
D) D) Turn property over to the desk officer
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 218-01

P.G. 218-01 requires the first officer to safeguard property in place until detective arrival.

Front:
**78.** A FINEST message must be transmitted for:

A) A) All arrests
B) B) Missing persons
C) C) Stolen vehicles valued over $1,000
D) D) All of the above
Back:
**Answer: D**

Source: P.G. 212-59

P.G. 212-59 mandates FINEST messages for all arrests, missing persons, and stolen vehicles valued over $1,000.

Front:
**79.** The desk officer must notify the Operations Unit for which of the following?

A) A) Any arrest involving a firearm
B) B) An unusual occurrence
C) C) A minor property crime
D) D) A routine aided case
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 212-09

P.G. 212-09 requires the desk officer to notify the Operations Unit for unusual occurrences.

Front:
**80.** Under P.G. 212-95, roll call training is conducted by:

A) A) The commanding officer
B) B) The platoon commander or designee
C) C) The training sergeant
D) D) Any available supervisor
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 212-95

P.G. 212-95 assigns responsibility for conducting roll call training to the training sergeant.

Front:
**81.** When a member of the service is the subject of a CCRB complaint, the desk officer must:

A) A) Investigate the complaint
B) B) Notify the member's commanding officer
C) C) Forward the complaint to IAB
D) D) Enter the complaint in the Command Log and notify the CO
Back:
**Answer: D**

Source: P.G. 207-17

When a MOS is the subject of a CCRB complaint, the desk officer must document it in the Command Log and notify the commanding officer.

Front:
**82.** A vehicle stop based on reasonable suspicion is classified as a:

A) A) Level 1 encounter
B) B) Level 2 encounter
C) C) Level 3 encounter
D) D) Arrest
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 208-01

Under P.G. 208-01, a vehicle stop based on reasonable suspicion constitutes a Level 3 encounter.

Front:
**83.** The RIGHT TO KNOW BUSINESS CARD must be offered during:

A) A) All arrests
B) B) Vehicle checkpoints and stops
C) C) Criminal investigations only
D) D) When requested by the subject
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-16

P.G. 221-16 requires the Right to Know Business Card to be offered during vehicle checkpoints and stops.

Front:
**84.** Which of the following is NOT an authorized use of O.C. pepper spray?

A) A) Gaining control of a person actively resisting arrest
B) B) Preventing an individual from injuring themselves
C) C) Controlling a subject who is passively resisting
D) D) Controlling a dangerous animal
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-07

P.G. 221-07 prohibits O.C. pepper spray on persons who are passively resisting, reserving it for active resistance or aggression.

Front:
**85.** When a UMOS discharges a firearm outside of NYC, the Operations Unit must be notified within:

A) A) 1 hour
B) B) 6 hours
C) C) 12 hours
D) D) 24 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-04

P.G. 221-04 requires the Operations Unit to be notified within 6 hours when a UMOS discharges a firearm outside NYC.

Front:
**86.** Under the Citywide Incident Management System, what are the four resource statuses?

A) A) Available, Assigned, Out-of-Service, Staged
B) B) Active, Reserve, Deployed, Standby
C) C) Ready, Committed, Released, Unavailable
D) D) On-scene, En-route, Staged, Demobilized
Back:
**Answer: A**

Source: P.G. 220-01

Under CIMS, the four resource statuses are Available, Assigned, Out-of-Service, and Staged.

Front:
**87.** The CIMS General Staff consists of:

A) A) Operations, Planning, Logistics, Finance/Administration
B) B) Operations, Intelligence, Communications, Support
C) C) Command, Operations, Planning, Support
D) D) Patrol, Detective, Support, Administrative
Back:
**Answer: A**

Source: P.G. 220-01

The CIMS General Staff consists of Operations, Planning, Logistics, and Finance/Administration Section Chiefs.

Front:
**88.** In CIMS, who is responsible for developing the Incident Action Plan?

A) A) Operations Section Chief
B) B) Planning Section Chief
C) C) Incident Commander
D) D) Logistics Section Chief
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-01

The Planning Section Chief is responsible for collecting information and preparing the Incident Action Plan.

Front:
**89.** A summons may be issued in lieu of arrest for:

A) A) Any felony
B) B) Certain misdemeanors and violations only
C) C) All crimes
D) D) Only traffic violations
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 209-01

P.G. 209-01 limits summons in lieu of arrest to certain misdemeanors and violations only.

Front:
**90.** When serving a summons, the officer must:

A) A) Arrest the individual if they refuse to sign
B) B) Explain the summons and obtain signature; if refused, note refusal
C) C) Leave the summons at the person's residence
D) D) Mail the summons within 24 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 209-01

P.G. 209-01 requires the officer to explain the summons and obtain signature; if refused, note the refusal.

Front:
**91.** The primary duty of all MOS when responding to an EDP is to:

A) A) Effect arrest
B) B) Preserve human life
C) C) Protect property
D) D) Gather evidence
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 states the primary duty of all MOS at an EDP scene is to preserve human life.

Front:
**92.** If an EDP is contained and believed armed/violent but poses no immediate threat, the supervisor should:

A) A) Attempt to subdue the EDP
B) B) Deploy tear gas
C) C) Take no additional action without CO/duty captain authorization
D) D) Order ESU to breach immediately
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-13

P.G. 221-13 directs the supervisor to take no additional action without CO/duty captain authorization when an EDP is contained but not an immediate threat.

Front:
**93.** The return of a UMOS to their permanent command after a firearms discharge causing death or injury requires approval of:

A) A) The Chief of Department
B) B) The patrol borough commander
C) C) The First Deputy Commissioner
D) D) The Police Commissioner
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-04

P.G. 221-04 requires First Deputy Commissioner approval for return to permanent command after a firearms discharge causing death or injury.

Front:
**94.** A supervisory officer is MANDATED to contact the Counseling Services Unit when a member is:

A) A) Under criminal investigation only
B) B) Placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to unfitness
C) C) Transferred to another command
D) D) Issued command discipline
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 318-10

A.G. 318-10 mandates supervisory contact with Counseling Services when a member is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed.

Front:
**95.** A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI will be charged with:

A) A) A.G. 304-04, "Fitness For Duty"
B) B) A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct"
C) C) A.G. 318-10, "Removal of Firearms"
D) D) P.G. 221-23, "Alcohol Testing"
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 304-04

A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI is charged with A.G. 304-06, Prohibited Conduct.

Front:
**96.** POPPA is best described as:

A) A) A Department disciplinary unit
B) B) A joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program
C) C) A civilian oversight board
D) D) An Internal Affairs Bureau division
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 304-04

POPPA is a joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program offering confidential peer support.

Front:
**97.** The determination of "reasonable time" for counsel before interrogation is made by:

A) A) The member's commanding officer
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) The captain (or above) in charge of the investigation
D) D) The member's union representative
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 318-11

A.G. 318-11 specifies the captain (or above) in charge of the investigation determines reasonable time for counsel.

Front:
**98.** When a UMOS is armed in NYC while off duty, the member may be unarmed if there is a likelihood of:

A) A) Working late
B) B) Attending a meeting
C) C) Consuming alcohol
D) D) Shopping
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 lists likelihood of consuming alcohol as a specific circumstance permitting a UMOS to be unarmed off duty in NYC.

Front:
**99.** How many forms must the investigating supervisor prepare when a civilian member is injured on duty?

A) A) 3
B) B) 4
C) C) 5
D) D) 6
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 319-08

A.G. 319-08 requires the investigating supervisor to prepare 5 forms when a civilian member is injured on duty.

Front:
**100.** A UMOS wishing to retain a Department-issued service firearm (post-1/15/13) must obtain a NICS check within how many days before separation?

A) A) 15 days
B) B) 30 days
C) C) 60 days
D) D) 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 requires a NICS check no more than 30 days prior to separation for retaining a Department-issued firearm.

Front:
**101.** Under vehicle checkpoint rules, stops must be conducted in a:

A) A) Random manner at the officer's discretion
B) B) Non-arbitrary manner (e.g., every third vehicle)
C) C) Manner targeting specific vehicle types
D) D) Way that maximizes the number of stops
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-16

P.G. 221-16 mandates vehicle checkpoint stops be conducted in a non-arbitrary manner, such as every third vehicle.

Front:
**102.** At a vehicle checkpoint, at least how many Department vehicles are required?

A) A) 1
B) B) 2
C) C) 3
D) D) 4
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-16

P.G. 221-16 requires a minimum of two Department vehicles at any vehicle checkpoint.

Front:
**103.** A supervisor must remain at a vehicle checkpoint for:

A) A) The first hour
B) B) The entire operation
C) C) Spot checks only
D) D) Until relieved by the desk officer
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-16

P.G. 221-16 requires the supervisor to remain at the checkpoint for the entire operation.

Front:
**104.** When property is invoiced and the value exceeds $5,000, notification must be made to:

A) A) The desk officer only
B) B) The commanding officer and Operations Unit
C) C) Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) The Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 218-01

P.G. 218-01 requires notification to the commanding officer and Operations Unit when invoiced property exceeds $5,000.

Front:
**105.** Under P.G. 216-01, when an aided case involves a person in need of medical assistance, the FIRST action is to:

A) A) Prepare an AIDED REPORT
B) B) Request an ambulance
C) C) Administer appropriate first aid
D) D) Notify the desk officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 216-01

P.G. 216-01 directs the responding member to immediately administer appropriate first aid as the first action for a person in need.

Front:
**106.** A CEW exposure beyond how many seconds may increase the risk of death or serious injury?

A) A) 5 seconds
B) B) 10 seconds
C) C) 15 seconds
D) D) 20 seconds
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-08

P.G. 221-08 states CEW exposure beyond 15 seconds may increase the risk of death or serious physical injury.

Front:
**107.** The RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW must be distributed within:

A) A) 24 hours
B) B) 3 business days
C) C) 5 business days
D) D) 10 business days
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 329-07

A.G. 329-07 requires the Resignation and Exit Interview to be distributed within 5 business days.

Front:
**108.** Operations Unit personnel notify all of the following upon learning of a resignation EXCEPT:

A) A) Communications Section
B) B) Internal Affairs Bureau
C) C) Payroll Section
D) D) The member's union representative
Back:
**Answer: D**

Source: A.G. 329-07

Upon learning of a resignation, Operations Unit notifies Communications Section, IAB, Payroll, and others — but not the union representative.

Front:
**109.** If a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report must be forwarded to Medical Division within:

A) A) 30 days
B) B) 60 days
C) C) 90 days
D) D) 120 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires a report to Medical Division within 60 days if LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously.

Front:
**110.** If a LOD injury comes under FECA, the CO forwards documentation to Medical Division within:

A) A) 30 days
B) B) 60 days
C) C) 90 days
D) D) 180 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-03

A.G. 330-03 requires FECA documentation to be forwarded to Medical Division within 90 days.

Front:
**111.** When multiple individuals are exposed to an infectious disease at a scene, documentation is coordinated by:

A) A) The desk officer
B) B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
C) C) The first member to arrive
D) D) Emergency Medical Services
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 330-09

A.G. 330-09 designates the patrol supervisor as coordinator for documenting multiple exposures to infectious disease.

Front:
**112.** Department computers are intended for:

A) A) Personal and official business
B) B) Official Department business only
C) C) Any lawful purpose
D) D) Official business during tours and personal use after hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 325-35

A.G. 325-35 states Department computers are intended for official Department business only.

Front:
**113.** The Department may review material on computer systems:

A) A) Only with prior notice to the member
B) B) Only during the member's assigned tour
C) C) At any time, with or without notice
D) D) Only with a court order
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 325-35

A.G. 325-35 reserves the Department's right to review computer systems at any time, with or without notice.

Front:
**114.** Under P.G. 213-01, when a mobilization is ordered, the desk officer must:

A) A) Wait for written confirmation
B) B) Immediately notify all members in the command
C) C) Make a Command Log entry and comply with mobilization plan
D) D) Contact the Chief of Patrol for authorization
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 213-01

P.G. 213-01 requires the desk officer to make a Command Log entry and comply with the mobilization plan.

Front:
**115.** Force incident levels do NOT apply to injuries sustained by:

A) A) Prisoners
B) B) Civilians
C) C) Members of the service
D) D) Bystanders
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 221-06

P.G. 221-06 states force incident levels do not apply to injuries sustained by members of the service.

Front:
**116.** The Trauma Counseling Team will NOT:

A) A) Respond to the station house
B) B) Visit the place of occurrence
C) C) Interfere with ongoing investigations
D) D) Contact members for follow-up
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-07

A.G. 330-07 states the Trauma Counseling Team will not interfere with ongoing investigations.

Front:
**117.** Under A.G. 305-07, a UMOS carrying a firearm into a foreign country without authorization faces:

A) A) A verbal warning
B) B) No consequences if properly secured
C) C) Disciplinary action and possibly criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation
D) D) Modified assignment only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 305-07

A.G. 305-07 warns that carrying a firearm into a foreign country without authorization may result in disciplinary action, criminal penalties, and weapon confiscation.

Front:
**118.** When is a Unified Command established under CIMS?

A) A) When the Police Commissioner directs
B) B) When multiple agencies or jurisdictions are involved
C) C) For all Level 1 mobilizations
D) D) Only during natural disasters
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-01

Unified Command is established when multiple agencies or jurisdictions share incident command responsibility.

Front:
**119.** Canine use at non-peaceful demonstrations requires prior approval of:

A) A) The borough commander
B) B) The Chief of Patrol
C) C) The Chief of Department
D) D) The duty captain
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-18

P.G. 221-18 requires prior approval from the Chief of Patrol before canine teams may be used at non-peaceful demonstrations.

Front:
**120.** Terminal leave computation beyond 20 years of service grants:

A) A) 1 day per completed year
B) B) 2 days per completed year
C) C) 3 days per completed year of service
D) D) 5 days per completed year
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 329-06

A.G. 329-06 provides that terminal leave beyond 20 years grants 3 days per completed year of service.

Front:
**121.** Upon learning of a mobilization for a disorder, a sergeant should:

A) A) Proceed directly to the mobilization point
B) B) 10-02 (report to station house) first, then go to mobilization
C) C) Notify the duty captain before responding
D) D) Wait for the desk officer to direct response
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 213-05

P.G. 213-05 (Duties at Disorder) requires 10-02 first, then report to the mobilization point. P.G. 213-03 (Rapid Mobilization) says go direct — the exam is guided by the governing procedure, and duties at a disorder fall under 213-05.

Front:
**122.** A UMOS finds a lost child. What is the FIRST step?

A) A) Prepare a Lost Person Report
B) B) Notify the Desk Officer/counterpart AND the Radio Dispatcher
C) C) Attempt to locate a parent or guardian
D) D) Notify the Missing Persons Squad
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 215-01

The first step when a UMOS finds a lost child is to notify the Desk Officer/counterpart AND the Radio Dispatcher — the exam tests "first step" language.

Front:
**123.** At a CBRN incident with suspected terrorism, who has primary agency for overall site management?

A) A) FDNY
B) B) NYPD
C) C) OEM
D) D) DHS
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 220-02, 220-03

NYPD has the Core Competency for Overall Site Management and Assessment/Investigation at CBRN incidents with suspected terrorism. FDNY has primary for life safety/fire.

Front:
**124.** A UMOS writes a "C" summons instead of an OATH for a CJRA violation. A patrol supervisor must respond:

A) A) True — supervisor must respond to all "C" summons
B) B) False — supervisor must respond only for Code 3 (law enforcement exception)
C) C) False — supervisor never responds to OATH/CJRA summons
D) D) True — but only if the defendant requests it
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 209-03, 209-12

The supervisor must respond ONLY for Code 3 (law enforcement exception). If the officer used Code 1, 2, or 4, no supervisor response is required. The exam tests whether you know the rule is specific to Code 3.

Front:
**125.** A vehicle collision involves a city vehicle and a private vehicle where the city may be liable. The UMOS checks the PAR back "Duplicate Copy Required For" box for:

A) A) Police Department
B) B) Comptroller
C) C) Law Department
D) D) Risk Management
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 217-04

For city vehicle collisions where the city may be liable, the "Duplicate Copy Required For" box for Comptroller must be checked. Persons involved must be informed they must file claims with the Comptroller within 90 days.

Front:
**126.** A prisoner at a hospital asks the guarding officer to remove handcuffs. The doctor then asks the officer to leave the room. The officer should:

A) A) Remove the cuffs only after the doctor examines the prisoner
B) B) Maintain visual contact from inside the room while the doctor examines
C) C) Step outside the room and wait in the hallway
D) D) Request a patrol supervisor before doing anything
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: P.G. 210-02

If the doctor asks the officer to leave the room, the officer does NOT need a PS — just step outside and maintain visual contact. The PS requirement only applies if the doctor wants the cuffs removed.

Front:
**127.** A supervisor discovers offensive graffiti in the locker room. What is the FIRST action?

A) A) Remove it immediately
B) B) Photograph it and secure/remove/cover it
C) C) Report it to IAB
D) D) Voucher it as found property
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 332-19

The first action is to photograph it and take immediate steps to secure/remove/cover it. Then voucher as "Investigatory Evidence," make a CLE (without describing the material), and notify the C.O.

Front:
**128.** A member of the service is discourteous to a civilian asking for directions. The correct answer under NYPD values:

A) A) No violation if the officer was busy
B) B) Cites the values statement on courtesy and civility
C) C) Only a formal complaint warrants action
D) D) Depends on whether the civilian files a complaint
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 200-02

This exact scenario appeared on a recent Sergeant exam — the correct answer cites the values statement on courtesy and civility. All members must be courteous to the public.

Front:
**129.** An officer responds to an elderly woman in a nursing home who was burned by hot coffee. What is the correct classification?

A) A) Routine sick — no aided report required
B) B) Injury — aided report required
C) C) Quality of life complaint
D) D) Medical emergency only — no report required
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 216-01

A past test had an elderly woman burnt by hot coffee — this is an injury, not a routine sick case, and requires an Aided Report. Routine sick applies only when no injury, police service, investigation, or notifications are needed.

Front:
**130.** For a DWI suspect arrested after a collision who refuses a breathalyzer, a chemical test warrant CAN be applied for when:

A) A) The suspect was the only person injured
B) B) Another person (a passenger) suffered serious physical injury
C) C) The suspect has prior DWI convictions
D) D) The arrest occurred on a highway
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 217-16

A chemical test warrant CAN be applied for because another person (the passenger) suffered serious physical injury. If the suspect had been the only injured party, no warrant would be possible under the serious physical injury standard.

Front:
**131.** Where does a lawyer fall in the hospital prisoner visit hierarchy (210-02)?

A) A) Interviewer
B) B) Visitor only
C) C) Neither — lawyers are not listed
D) D) Emergency contact
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 210-02

In 210-02 (hospital), a lawyer is a Visitor, NOT an Interviewer. In 210-01 (precinct/BCS), the lawyer is an Interviewer. This is a major exam distinction.

Front:
**132.** A vehicle owner requests a private tow truck after a DARP request was already made. The member must:

A) A) Grant the request if the tow arrives faster
B) B) Deny the request — DARP cannot be cancelled once requested
C) C) Allow the private tow if the owner signs a waiver
D) D) Let the owner decide between the two
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 217-09

Once a DARP request is made, it cannot be cancelled — except for listed exemptions. The vehicle owner's private preference is NOT an exemption. The member must deny the request.

Front:
**133.** A member drives a Department vehicle, runs a red light without slowing, and causes a collision. The appropriate action is:

A) A) Retraining
B) B) Discipline (unsafe disregard)
C) C) Verbal warning
D) D) No action if no injury resulted
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 217-06

Running a red light without slowing constitutes unsafe disregard and warrants discipline. If a member skids on ice and loses control, the appropriate action is retraining (driving deficiency/ability factor), not discipline.

Front:
**134.** At an EDP scene, a sergeant should FIRST establish:

A) A) A perimeter
B) B) Firearms control
C) C) A command post
D) D) Communication with the EDP
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: P.G. 221-13

At an EDP scene, firearms control must be established immediately. Life safety is the primary duty, but firearms must be controlled first to prevent escalation.

Front:
**135.** A sergeant evaluates a probationary officer. At the 16th month evaluation, the rating is below competent. What happens next?

A) A) Immediate termination
B) B) 22nd month evaluation with detailed Typed Letterhead to Performance Analysis Unit
C) C) Extension of probation up to 6 months
D) D) Transfer to another command
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 331-02

A below-competent rating at 16th month triggers an extension of probation up to 6 additional months. The 22nd month evaluation with Typed Letterhead to PAU is for a Termination recommendation, not a below-competent rating alone.

Front:
**136.** A member files an EEO complaint. A supervisor must report a discriminatory act by:

A) A) Email only
B) B) Telephone only
C) C) In person only
D) D) Any method — no specific requirement
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 332-14

A supervisor must report discriminatory acts by TELEPHONE only. A complainant (non-supervisor) can call, email, fax, write, or do it in person — but supervisors have a specific telephone-only requirement.

Front:
**137.** An EEO complaint filing deadline runs from:

A) A) Date of the original report
B) B) Date of occurrence
C) C) Date the complainant first informed a supervisor
D) D) Date of the investigation conclusion
Back:
**Answer: B**

Source: A.G. 332-14

Filing deadlines run from the date of occurrence, not the date of the original report or investigation conclusion. This is measured from when the discriminatory act happened.

Front:
**138.** To leave the city while out sick, a UMOS must request permission through:

A) A) Chief of Personnel
B) B) Sick Desk Supervisor
C) C) Desk Officer, permanent command
D) D) District Surgeon
Back:
**Answer: D**

Source: A.G. 330-03

The District Surgeon grants approval for out-of-city travel while on sick. The Chief of Personnel grants the approval, but the request goes through the District Surgeon.

Front:
**139.** A UMOS going out on regular sick should provide the desk officer at the permanent command with:

A) A) Name of Sick Desk member notified and time notified
B) B) Name, time, and sick serial number
C) C) Sick serial number only
D) D) All three: name, time, and serial number
Back:
**Answer: C**

Source: A.G. 330-03

UMOS gets 3 pieces of information from the Sick Desk (name, time notified, serial number) but only passes the sick serial number to the desk officer. The exam tests this specific detail.

Front:
**140.** An officer is arrested for domestic violence. Under PG 208-37, no notification is required. But under PG 212-32:

A) A) The Desk Officer must notify the Captain
B) B) The officer self-reports
C) C) No action is required
D) D) IAB must be notified
Back:
**Answer: A**

Source: P.G. 208-37 vs 212-32

PG 208-37 says no notification for off-duty incidents, BUT PG 212-32 says the Desk Officer must notify the Captain. Both procedures exist — a sergeant must know both.
